methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
GalaxEye is a Series A–stage space & aerospace company founded by Suyash Singh and Kishan Thakkar that has raised $10M to date. The valuation universe includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers ranging from SpaceX to emerging ventures like Apex Space, though GalaxEye's narrow positioning within this sector limits direct peer overlap.

Given data constraints, only the stage-progression method is applicable. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement yields an implied post-money valuation of $20M. Public comparables in the sector trade at a 25.8x median P/S multiple across 6 tickers, but this approach cannot be applied without GalaxEye's revenue figure—standard for private companies. Peer-median triangulation was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar characteristics (0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage peers identified, below the 3-point minimum threshold).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M–$23M, centered on $20M. This range reflects stage-typical progression assumptions but carries material uncertainty given single-method triangulation and the absence of revenue-based anchoring or sufficient private peer granularity. Key risks include GalaxEye's specific market traction, capital efficiency relative to sector peers, and technology differentiation—none of which are quantified in available data. Additional disclosure of near-term revenue or customer metrics would materially strengthen conviction.
